---
title: "3 months"
author: "OMKAR"
date: "2025-11-06"
output: word_document
---


```{r }
# -------------------------------
# Data for T = 3 Month Expiry
# -------------------------------

So <- c(978.4665, 978.4665, 978.4665,988.35, 988.35, 988.35,998.2335, 998.2335)
K <- c(968.583, 988.35, 1008.117,968.583, 988.35, 1008.117,968.583, 988.35)
r <- c(0.045, 0.055, 0.065,0.055, 0.065, 0.045,0.065, 0.045)
sigma <- c(0.15, 0.2, 0.25,0.25, 0.15, 0.2,0.2, 0.25)
BS <- c(40.21045195, 40.4220316, 42.25540863,66.27750015, 37.65727248, 35.14294582,65.26672271, 59.98612722)

# Combine into dataframe
data <- data.frame(So, K, r, sigma, BS)
print(data)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Calculate Signal-to-Noise Ratio (Larger-the-better)
# Because higher BS value = better option value
# Formula: S/N = -10 * log10(mean(1 / y^2))
#-----------------------------------------------------------

data$SN_ratio <- -10 * log10(1 / (data$BS^2))
print(data)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Analyze mean S/N ratios for each factor
#-----------------------------------------------------------

So_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$So), FUN = mean)
colnames(So_SN) <- c("So", "Mean_SN")

K_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$K), FUN = mean)
colnames(K_SN) <- c("K", "Mean_SN")

r_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$r), FUN = mean)
colnames(r_SN) <- c("r", "Mean_SN")

sigma_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$sigma), FUN = mean)
colnames(sigma_SN) <- c("sigma", "Mean_SN")

print(So_SN)
print(K_SN)
print(r_SN)
print(sigma_SN)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Plot main effects for each parameter
#-----------------------------------------------------------

par(mfrow=c(2,2))
# So
plot(1:3, So_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "red",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Stock Price (So)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - So")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = So_SN$So)

# K
plot(1:3, K_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "green",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Strike Price (K)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - K")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = K_SN$K)

# r
plot(1:3, r_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Interest Rate (r)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - r")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = r_SN$r)

# sigma
plot(1:3, sigma_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "purple",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Volatility (σ)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - σ")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = sigma_SN$sigma)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Identify best combination (highest S/N ratio)
#-----------------------------------------------------------

best_So <- So_SN$So[which.max(So_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_K <- K_SN$K[which.max(K_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_r <- r_SN$r[which.max(r_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_sigma <- sigma_SN$sigma[which.max(sigma_SN$Mean_SN)]

cat("Best combination:\n")
cat("So =", best_So, "\n")
cat("K =", best_K, "\n")
cat("r =", best_r, "\n")
cat("sigma =", best_sigma, "\n")

# Interpretation:
# The combination with the **highest S/N ratio** gives the 
# most stable and optimal (largest) Black–Scholes option value.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Calculate Signal-to-Noise Ratio (Larger-the-Better)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
data$SN_ratio <- -10 * log10(1 / (data$BS^2))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Function to Generate Response Table for Any Metric
# ------------------------------------------------------------
generate_response_table <- function(data, response_col) {
  factors <- c("So", "K", "r", "sigma")
  tables <- list()
  
  for (factor in factors) {
    means <- aggregate(data[[response_col]], by = list(data[[factor]]), FUN = mean)
    colnames(means) <- c("Level", "Mean")
    delta <- max(means$Mean) - min(means$Mean)
    tables[[factor]] <- data.frame(Factor = factor, Delta = round(delta, 4))
  }
  
  result <- do.call(rbind, tables)
  result$Rank <- rank(-result$Delta)  # Rank by descending Delta
  return(result)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Generate Tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------
sn_table <- generate_response_table(data, "SN_ratio")
mean_table <- generate_response_table(data, "BS")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Display Results
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\nResponse Table for Signal-to-Noise Ratios (Larger-the-Better)\n")
print(sn_table, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nResponse Table for Means\n")
print(mean_table, row.names = FALSE)

```
